summary:
Yes, several small-molecule CGRP receptor antagonists such as ubrogepant and atogepant are already approved and clinically used for treating migraines. This confirms active development and real-world efficacy of these targeted therapies.  

Note: The summary must not include any external information or references beyond what was provided by Agent2’s response.
